findings.description: charge_order logs the full card_number AND cvv in plaintext at INFO level — a textbook PCI-DSS violation (CVV must never be stored/logged in any form post-auth).

Transaction id derives from Python's per-process-randomized hash() truncated to 10 digits, so tx ids are neither reproducible across runs nor reliably unique (collision-prone), violating the 'unique transaction id' contract.
charge_order never checks whether the order is already 'paid', so calling it twice charges the customer twice — no idempotency guard despite the status column being available.

The card is charged before the DB write is guaranteed: if the UPDATE/commit on lines 80-84 raises (db locked, disk full), money is taken but the order is never marked paid, leaving a charged-but-unrecorded order.
Money is modeled as float (unit_price, TAX_RATE, the *100 cents conversion via round()), guaranteeing binary-float penny drift; currency must use Decimal or integer cents.

No try/except around gateway.charge, which the docstring calls a wrapper over an upstream SDK; a network/timeout error becomes an unhandled traceback to the caller instead of a ChargeResult(ok=False).

monthly_revenue_report computes revenue as unit_price*quantity*100 with no discount and no tax, contradicting compute_order_total (which applies both), so reported 'revenue' disagrees with the amount actually charged.
discount_pct is never validated/clamped to 0..100, so a discount >100 yields negative line totals and a negative discount silently marks the price up.
compute_order_total returns an unrounded float total (tax multiplication after per-line rounding), so the returned dollar amount can carry sub-cent noise even though amount_cents is later rounded.
PaymentGateway accepts and threads raw card_number/cvv through method signatures and the ChargeResult flow with no tokenization boundary, maximizing PAN/CVV exposure surface in memory and logs.
report['items'] sums item quantities while the docstring says 'per-order item counts' — ambiguous whether it counts line items or units.
